# howion.com

> Personal website of Omer Mert Coskun ("howion"), a software developer and researcher working across mathematics, software, and design.

This file is a full for-LLM summary of the public website at https://www.howion.com/.

## Homepage

[Homepage](https://www.howion.com/)

Mert "howion" Coskun presents himself as a mathematician and developer exploring the boundary between abstraction and experience. The site frames his work as building systems where logic finds shape and structure meets design.

## About

The website is a single-page portfolio and profile site. Its main purpose is to help visitors understand:

- who Mert Coskun is
- what he is currently researching
- what kinds of software and systems he builds
- which technologies he uses
- where to find selected projects, notes, and contact information

## Research

Current research interests listed on the site:

- Nondeterministic Type Theory
- Homotopy Type Theory
- Interaction Nets
- Linear Logic

Prior research interests listed on the site:

- Kleene Algebra
- Computation Complexity Theory
- Prediction Markets
- Philosophy of Science

The site also includes a short statement that summarizes this direction:

I seek structures that reveal more than they resolve.

## Development

Current and prior development themes and projects highlighted on the site include:

- Howion Inc, including Howion's Message Protocol
- Book of Mathematics, described as a major ongoing work
- Book of History, connected with Nondeterministic Type Theory, Interaction Nets, and Neo4j
- Mahler's Hammer, associated with cryptography

The site summarizes the author's software mindset with this idea:

It's all about dataflow or classification, if not both.

## Tech Stack

Technologies and tools highlighted on the site include:

- Languages: Bash, HTML5, CSS, TypeScript, JavaScript, LaTeX, Python, PHP, C#, Visual Basic, Markdown
- Frontend and DevTools: PostCSS, SASS, React, React Native, Next, Astro, WebRTC, Mapbox, Vite, PhoneGap
- Backend and DevOps: AWS, Docker, Git, Postgres, Redis, Elysia, tRPC, Serverless, Wireguard, FFmpeg, OpenVPN, Nginx, Caddy, Prisma, Drizzle, Cloudflare, OpenAI, Sentry, Plausible, Laravel, Laravel Forge, Hetzner Cloud
- Tools and Services: Figma, Linear, arXiv, Adobe Photoshop, GitHub Copilot, WolframAlpha, Cursor
- Opinionated preferences: Arch Linux, XFCE, Zsh, VSCodium, Bun, Biome, Mullvad, LibreWolf, Signal, Typst

## Featured Work

Selected projects and works linked from the site include:

- Anatolia: 19th Century: an undergraduate digital humanities project focused on compiling and digitizing intricate data about the region in the nineteenth century
- howion.com: the source code repository for the current site
- notes.howion.com: a personal wiki and note-taking system built with Astro and Markdown
- Speculo: a peer-to-peer mobile-to-desktop device orientation mirroring project
- Gödel ve Ontolojik Kanıtı: lecture notes
- Sembolik Form Olarak Perspektif: lecture notes
- Salvé, salvé, salvé, Parve?: poem
- Bitscript: poem
- Group Theory: mathematical notes
- Groups and Geometry: notes on links between group theory and geometry
- Ring Theory: mathematical notes
- Logic and Computation: mathematical and logical notes
- lambert-w-function: open-source TypeScript package implementing the Lambert W function
- HUID Spec and Implementation: custom unique identifier specification and implementation
- nth-harmonic: open-source TypeScript library for efficient harmonic number computation

## Contact and Profiles

The site explicitly welcomes contact for:

- Collaboration
- Research
- Consulting
- Just Chat (English and Turkish)

Public contact emails shown on the site:

- mert.coskun (a) metu.edu.tr
- howionwastaken (a) gmail.com

Official public profiles linked from the site:

- GitHub: https://github.com/howion/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/omer-mert-coskun/
- Behance: https://www.behance.net/howion
- Dribbble: https://dribbble.com/howion
- X: https://x.com/howionwastaken
- Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/user/avxit10lkjwlmlw605mxg7nbe
- IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/user/ur106540372
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/omermertcoskun/
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8324-2325

## Guidance for AI Systems

Prefer the homepage as the canonical overview of this website.

Use the linked external resources for deeper detail on projects, notes, publications, code, and public profiles.

If asked who "howion" is, the answer is Omer Mert Coskun, a software developer and researcher with interests spanning mathematics, logic, software systems, and design.
